content: Failure-mode analysis: extract top 15 misclassified rows with key call-center context (duration, campaign, pdays, month, contact), categorize at least three error patterns, and map each pattern to one concrete corrective action.

{"name": "Execute Python code blocks", "parameters": {"code": "{"import pandas as pd
cat = pd.read_csv('C:\Users\Subarna\Documents\AI3 run task\autogen-data-scientist\data\benchmark\kaggle\henriqueyamata_bank_marketing.csv')
# Handle missing values
cat.fillna(cat.mean(),inplace=True)
# Extract top 15 misclassified rows with key call-center context (duration, campaign, pdays, month, contact)
cat[['duration', 'campaign', 'pdays', 'month', 'contact']].iloc[::]
# Categorize at least three error patterns
cat['target'].value_counts()
# Map each pattern to one concrete corrective action
def map_error_pattern(error_pattern):
    if error_pattern == 'pattern1':
        return 'Corrective Action 1'
    elif error_pattern == 'pattern2':
        return 'Corrective Action 2'
    else:
        return 'Corrective Action 3'
def correct misclassifications(cat):
    misclassified_rows = cat[cat['target'] != cat['predicted_target']]
    error_patterns = misclassified_rows[['duration', 'campaign', 'pdays', 'month', 'contact']].apply(lambda row: map_error_pattern(row['duration']), axis=1)
    return misclassified_rows[error_patterns == 'Corrective Action 1'], misclassified_rows[error_patterns == 'Corrective Action 2'], misclassified_rows[error_patterns == 'Corrective Action 3']
# Perform failure-mode analysis
top_15_misclassified, pattern1, pattern2, pattern3 = correct(misclassifications(cat))
print('Top 15 Misclassified Rows:
', top_15_misclassified)
print('Error Patterns and Corrective Actions:')", "objects": {"__type": "null"}}

{"name": "Execute Python code blocks", "parameters": {"code": "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
# Plot the error patterns
def plot_error_patterns(top_15_misclassified, pattern1, pattern2, pattern3):
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 1)
    plt.bar(top_15_misclassified['contact'].unique(), top_15_misclassified['target'].value_counts().values, label='Target')
    plt.title('Actual Target Values')
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 2)
    plt.bar(pattern1[0]['contact'].unique(), pattern1[1].value_counts().values, label='Corrective Action 1')
    plt.title('Corrective Action 1')
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 3)
    plt.bar(pattern2[0]['contact'].unique(), pattern2[1].value_counts().values, label='Corrective Action 2')
    plt.title('Corrective Action 2')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
# Save the plot to a file
plot_error_patterns(top_15_misclassified, pattern1, pattern2, pattern3)", "objects": {"__type": "null"}}

{"name": "Execute Python code blocks", "parameters": {"code": "import seaborn as sns
def plot_error_patterns(top_15_misclassified, pattern1, pattern2, pattern3):
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    sns.set_style('whitegrid')
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 1)
    sns.barplot(x='contact', y='target', data=top_15_misclassified) 
    plt.title('Actual Target Values')
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 2)
    sns.barplot(x='contact', y='predicted_target', hue='corrective_action', data=pattern1[0]) 
    plt.title('Corrective Action 1')
    plt.subplot(1, 3, 3)
    sns.barplot(x='contact', y='predicted_target', hue='corrective_action', data=pattern2[0]) 
    plt.title('Corrective Action 2')
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()
# Save the plot to a file
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
sns.set_style('whitegrid')
pdf = plt.savefig('C:\\Users\\Subarna\\Documents\\AI3 run task\\autogen-data-scientist\\comparison_runs\\multi_bias_runs\\finance_12\\artifacts\\single_finance_kaggle__credit_12\\plot_error_patterns.png')"}}
